comments.title: Effects while practicing the sphere of protection

I'm not sure if it is because I am a much more "sensitive" person or not, but I catch myself joyously tearing up or even crying once I start the closing of the ceremony. I take it as a good thing, but I would love to have your thoughts or any of the commentariat's ideas on it.

replies.title: Re: Effects while practicing the sphere of protection
replies.content: That doesn't happen to me, but then I'm not a particularly emotional person; in Jungian terms, feeling is my weak or "inferior" function, the one I habitually use least. If emotion is your strong function, it's quite reasonable that you should have that reaction.

I hear you. My spatial sense if my weakest function. Ask me how something fits together and I'm like, duh! Executive functions also, if not weak, nothing to write home about. comments.title: Watcher at the Threshold and divination
comments.content:
Dear JMG,

So I have a big music project (for some one else) that I've been stalling on, even though over half has been recorded, and I'm proud of the dozens of hours of work that have gone into it so far. But I've just put off the next step for so long, always with a reasonable sounding reason... and as of this week, somehow a year just up and passed without any forward motion.

Despite a variety of personal dramas, I'm cognizant of the fact that my tardiness at this point is down to me and my own issues. It's been stalled for so long that I've kind of lost my mojo. Aware that this is still the Mercury Retrograde (apparently a good time to, say, revive and reinvigorate something previously begun), but somewhat doubting my ability to actually shift gears again now so late, I did a Tarot pull: If I make a concerted effort to get back to work on the project this week, can I make real progress? --Nine of Wands Reversed. Seems rather unlikely; I'll still be stuck in the grip of my own issues, apparently. I did a few follow up refinement questions; all achieved similarly negative results. Finally I asked, what is the likely result if I just quit this project entirely? 8 of Pentacles. ...What? That's the way to make progress at my craft?

Now, I see two possibilities here. One is that my time on this project has truly passed, my energies on it have stagnated, and despite the feelings of utter failure I would surely feel as a result, somehow it would be better for all, and finally allow me to move forward, to just let go of this project, apologize, and ask them to pass the rest of the job on to someone else.

I see another possibility, though. My question is about the Watcher at the Threshold. The kind of testing that the Watcher provides need not be explicitly in the realm of occult learning, yes? It can simply be in any realm which tests the strength of my will to exceed my current comfort zone, and it won't relent unless I prove myself able to surmount it. All of my travails this year which I let delay me could even potentially have been such tests, and if so, then so far I have been found wanting. So my question is:

Is it possible for the Watcher at the Threshold to mess with divinations in order to intentionally mislead, in order to test one's resolve and make certain that they really are ready to proceed? Or in other words, might I reasonably take this situation to be one where divination won't necessarily give me a straightforward answer, and I just need to come to a decision myself?

replies.title: Re: Watcher at the Threshold and divination
replies.content: Yes, the Watcher can mess with you, and mess with your divinations. In your case I'd make myself finish the project no matter what. This is one of the great challenges every creative person faces, and the only answer that works is to suck it up and make yourself finish the thing, no matter what excuses your mind offers you.

replies.title: Re: Watcher at the Threshold and divination
replies.content: Thank you JMG, you've given me a powerful reply. That truly helps me.

replies.title: Re: Watcher at the Threshold and divination
replies.content: Regarding the Watcher.  Could your Higher Self and Watcher be the same?  Constant block, test, yet there is guidance that you have to ultimately decide on

replies.title: Re: Watcher at the Threshold and divination
replies.content: Good! Yes, that's a teaching in some occult schools: the Watcher is the first manifestation of your Higher Self, and keeps you from getting in over your head until you're ready.

replies.content: What is the copper ring and mudball technique?

replies.title: (no subject)
replies.content:
A method for grounding fast that I made when I needed to get rid of unpleasant anxiety that had made its way to my body, repeated versions of this cleared it out. TCM calls it fire of different organs, for me it was heart and liver fire. The ring needs to be a spiral pointing out of the body, not a closed loop. You can make it, or braid it, out of common copper wire or buy it from an indian import shop.

https://ecosophia.dreamwidth.org/199059.html?thread=34555795#cmt34555795

For a quicker version of this, though specific for the heating part, drink cool, not cold, ash guard juice aka "winter melon". You can also apply castor oil in the points mentioned and go your way, or both. There are many things many people suggest to apply to these point with different effects, the ones I give below are meant to cool you down. Whatever you do, don't use any sort of ash as it would likely make it worse.

Materials:

1) A Copper ring
2) Soil

Steps:

1. Clean the copper ring with lime juice and salt. Metals shouldn't be worn when they have an oxide layer and particularly copper. Don't use soap or any synthetic cleaners. You can put the ring in a small container and fill it with lemon or lime juice, it will be mostly clean after 30 minutes. Then scrub it with salt to get the rest off or you could add lime and salt to a paper cloth and clean it with that if you don't want to wait. Tamarind paste also works. The lemon juice has probably changed color into a shade of green if the ring was dirty. Throw this away.

2. Ask an Earth or fertility goddess to bless the ring for grounding and removing bad energies whatever way you like or if you prefer, just ask the Earth itself. Venus is a good option but there are many. The ring should be worn on the left ring finger of the left hand. If it happens to be a copper snake ring, as they are popular, the head of the snake should point to the finger tips, not towards you. You can wear the ring for this only, or often if you clean it daily, don't sleep with it as it might leave a green stain if your skin PH is alkaline. If you forget, don't worry, just clean the stain off your finger the same way you did the ring.

3. Get a handful of soil from a pot or your garden, or other place where people don't walk on a regular basis, we want the energies of the earth undisturbed. Compost-soil works well just be sure it's fully composted and doesn't smell but of fresh soil.

4. Add clean water to the soil for it to be moist enough to hold a shape but not turn liquid.

5. Say a short prayer or just try to settle putting everything down mentally, emotionally and in the tension of your body. So basically relax or try to relax. Many times just trying to relax is more than enough in order to relax.

6. When you are ready, put a little bit of the mud in a plate or your left palm and add more water until it turns liquid. With the ring finger of the right hand put a generous amount of the soil mixture on the following points doing a slight massage in circles. Try both directions and also shifting your focus between your skin and your fingers. See what feels better, note if any of those feels unwinding. If it hurts, press a little harder and massage for a little more. The points are: an inch below your navel; where the rib cage meets; the pit of the throat; the forehead; and the soft spot behind the ears. To find the soft spot behind the ears place a finger on the back of your neck where it meets the skull, at the height of the ears. Move to either side towards the ear and when you feel the bone, just backtrack a little and put some mud paste on that soft spot on both sides.

7. Now, with your hands make a ball of mud and hold it with both hands for a bit in front of you comfortably. Focus on The Earth and follow your thoughts. For example: feel her generosity, your whole material body and the entire planet is made out of Earth in its milliards of combinations. She takes in filth and decay and produces life and harmony with it. If you feel dirty, worked up or unbalanced, you just have to reestablish this connection. This is a gift we are all given by the Earth.

8. Hold the mud ball tight, but not stiff on your hand and go and take an unplanned walk like that, with a mid-slow but steady pace, looking slightly downwards. Be conscious of the core of the planet pulling you as gravity. Try both hands for a few minutes and see what feels more relaxing for you. Yes, there is mud in your face. If it makes you uncomfortable that people might see you like that dust it off a little bit or wear a cap. We don't want to add any more stress, but if you can, leave as much of the mud as possible or just put a dot of mud paste in between your eye brows instead. Alternatively, you could pace up and down a hallway or other walking path, preferably close to a natural space.

9. Give thanks and wash away the mud ball on a river or on running water, burry it in your front yard or next to a big tree.

10. Go and do something else that you enjoy.

It's elaborate, but it works.

replies.title: (no subject)
replies.content: It's not really comparable. The GD rituals generally are much more focused, and designed to be done within the context of other rituals, rather than as standalone practices -- even the LRP is usually done along with the Middle Pillar as a daily practice. The SoP was specifically designed as a standalone practice, for those who don't plan on getting into ceremonial magic but want or need a basic protective, balancing, and energizing ritual for daily use. Comparing them is like saying, "Well, that's a nice canoe, but how does it compare to a bicycle and a Jeep?"

comments.title: Am I some kind of evil entity?
comments.content:
Hi JMG,

For as long as I can remember, I've been repelled by manifestations of faith, particularly Abrahamic faiths. There isn't a lot of sincere faith in the modern Western world, but when I see or am near some expression of strong religious faith, I experience a skin-crawling "get away" feeling.

I wasn't subjected to any kind of religion-related trauma or anything, I had a completely secular upbringing. I recall that when I learned about fundamentalist religious thinking I regarded it with derision, and when I was a kid I went through a phase where I would often watch religious TV shows to marvel at their absurdity. But I never felt that primal revulsion from any of this material, and on some level I recognized that there was no real faith behind any of it. But the instinctual religious abhorrence predated my phase of interest in religious pop culture, going back as early as I can remember.

My strongest experiences in this vein were when I traveled to less developed countries where religion is still an essential part of life for many people. Witnessing prayers in an ancient church filled me with an urge to sprint out the door, which I resisted just to amble outside "for some fresh air."

I've always felt an attraction to occult philosophy and symbolism, experiencing "magic" as something completely different from "faith." I've never had any interest in demonic or Satanic magical practices, however; all my knowledge indicates that that's a downward-spiraling path for the broken and miserable. Seeing the manifestations of its newfound popularity has only lessened my interest in it. Interestingly, there have been a couple recent occasions where I was in a place that bore adornments which I knew were related to serious, sincere infernal magical practice and I experienced a similar revulsion as towards "holy" things. These were the first times I'd ever witnessed signs of infernal practice firsthand; a sign of the times.

So what do you make of this? Ever hear from anyone else with experiences like these? Thanks for your time.

replies.title: Re: Am I some kind of evil entity?
replies.content: No, you're not some kind of evil entity, not least because you have an equal aversion to Satanist symbols. My guess is that you got burnt very badly by religion in your last incarnation and so came into this one with a hearty dislike of the whole phenomenon. If you take up serious magical training, you may find that your dislike fades; that was my experience, at least.

comments.title: Secrecy vs group workings
comments.content:
1) There has been a trend recently of people online working magic towards a common goal (e.g. money magic to profit from crypto investments) and sharing their workings, times of working and other details.

How does this sort of coordinated group work differ from the traditional requirement to keep your work secret? Is it still possible for magic to be effective if it is discussed openly beforehand (assuming, of course, that sharing plans does not cause some nefarious actor to work against those goals)?

2) Would the type of goal determine the spiritual powers invoked in a magical working? In other words, is it better to call upon an Elemental or spirit of the dead rather than an Angel for more material goals like wealth or job finding, or does it not matter? Would an Angel be less inclined to assist with money magic than a gnome (as an example)?

As always, thank you for taking the time to respond to these questions. This place really is a bastion of knowledge in a hellish realm of tiktok witch aesthetics and grubby demonolaters conjuring spirits from the currently fashionable grimoire for likes and followers.

A

replies.title: Re: Secrecy vs group workings
replies.content:
1) Heavily publicized workings of this kind have two downsides. The first, as you've noted, is that if someone disagrees with the intentions of such a working, they can get all the details and monkeywrench the working. The second is that talking about a working dissipates the force. This is something that's true in most creative pursuits; it's legendary among writers, for example, that talking about a story you're working on is the best way to make sure that the story never gets finished, because all the emotional force behind the story leaks away. This is why so many of these public workings fail so dismally.

2) Yes, very much so. If you want something spiritual, talk to an angel; if you want something on the plane of mind, talk to an intelligence; if you want something on the material plane, talk to a spirit. I don't recommend talking to the dead except in very limited circumstances, and I don't recommend interacting with demons at all, except to banish them -- whatever you do with them will always be turned against you.

It's quite common in some schools of magic, however, to work down the planes from the divine to the plane where you want the effect to take place. A Golden Dawn mage, for example, will start by invoking the Divine in some specific form, then call on the archangel, the angel, the intelligence, and finally the spirit, in order to make something happen on the material plane. It works very well, because the point is that you're fitting your intention into the natural pattern of the cosmos rather than trying to force it in all anyhow.

replies.title: Re: Secrecy vs group workings
replies.content:
Regarding not talking to the dead except in very limited circumstances - what would those circumstances be?

Even if you could find a legitimate medium, is it still better not to consult them?

replies.title: Re: Secrecy vs group workings
replies.content: If you have a family connection to someone dead, it's legitimate to pray to them and ask their help; that's the basis of ancestor worship. There's also prayer to saints (who are dead, after all) in some religions and inner plane contacts in magic, which is rather similar. Other than that, I don't recommend it -- and I don't recommend working with mediums at all.

replies.title: Re: Orgone
replies.content:
1) The best primary sources are of course Reich's own works, which are still very much in print; you might try The Function of the Orgasm, The Cancer Biopathy, The Bion Experiments, and The Orgone Energy Accumulator. There are many secondary sources, and I've read only a few of them; one I'd especially recommend is The Orgone Accumulator Handbook by James DeMeo, who's done quite a bit of first-rate research into Reich's discoveries.

2) Lots of things. Fluid condensers will pick up and hold energies charged with intention -- I use them to make talismans, for example, and for other purposes of practical magic. Orgone accumulators -- well, they've mostly been used for healing up to this point, but the same construction of alternate layers of organic and inorganic substances was used to create long and round barrows in ancient Britain, and in many other places. You can use the method to access other states of consciousness, to direct beneficial energies to crops and other nonhuman subjects, and any number of other things.

replies.title: Re: SOP with Tyr

Howdy Other Dave,

I invoke Anglo-Saxon Gods and Goddesses in the SOP in this way:

East/Air: Woden (Odin)
South/Fire: Frige (Freya)
West/Water: Ing (Frey)
North/Earth: Idun (Idun/Idunna)
Down/Spirit Below: Erce (Nerthus)
Up/Spirit Above: Tiw (Tyr)

This wasn't what I started with, but prayer, meditation, and divination led me to this. Originally, I had Thunor (Thor) in the East, Njord in the West, Frigg in the North, and Woden above, but I apparently needed to shuffle things around for my own practice.

If you were looking for other associations, a couple other to consider:
- Many folks interpret the Tiw (Tyr) stanza in the Anglo-Saxon Rune poem as referring to the North Star, which would be associated with the direction of the north, but maybe not so much with Earth
- In the interpretatio Romana and the days of the week, Tyr is associated with Mars, so some associations that work for Mars might work for Tyr as well (such as Fire/the South)

Hope these help, and good luck with the GSF work!
Jeff

comments.content: JMG, what are the events which make the foundation chart of a country invalid and give it a new foundational chart? Thanks!

replies.title: (no subject)
replies.content: The replacement of one constitution and system of government by another. In Germany, for example, the foundation chart of the German empire in 1871 was replaced by the foundation chart of the Weimar republic in 1918, and then by the foundation chart of the Bundesrepublik in 1949. The Nazi takeover in 1933 didn't replace the constitution -- Hitler just amended it and then ignored it -- and the collapse of the DDR in 1989 simply extended the power of what was then West Germany to the whole country.

replies.title: (no subject)
replies.content: But then the Russian Revolution called for a new foundation chart, and the fall of the soviet Union, to be replaced by the Russian Federation, called for another? And what date would you use for the latter?

replies.title: (no subject)
replies.content: Yes, exactly. The Soviet Union had a foundation chart, and the Russian Federation has its own, different foundation chart. The date, time, and place to use would be that of the formal establishment of the Russian Federation as a legal entity -- just as the foundation chart of the US is based on the date, time, and place of the signing of the Declaration of Independence, the legal document that brought the United States into being.

comments.title: Life Repair Using Magic
comments.content:
I've heard you talk about people turning their lives around by practicing magic, how does magic help in this regard?

In looking to commit to your book Learning Ritual Magic a few questions come up. Where does one go afterward? The Golden Dawn system? Also something Stephen Skinner reiterates, the difference between magic and mysticism..saying magic is for getting things done, practical applications and mysticism is totally different. His method of calling spirit before circle, issue charge, get thing asked for is understandable from the outside. Why do some magicians call GD magic quasi-mystical?

replies.title: Re: Life Repair Using Magic
replies.content:
1) Most of the difficulties in life are caused by inadequate will and inadequate imagination. Magic teaches you how to develop and use both of these.

2) If you visit the Magic Monday FAQ, which is linked above, you'll find a list of books in which Learning Ritual Magic is the first step. As for Skinner's definition, you'll find that there's a lot of dispute about what counts or doesn't count as magic. I don't worry about such things too much; by my definition, there's practical magic, aimed at making things happen in the world, and spiritual magic, aimed at making things happen within yourself, and an effective magical system includes both.

In addition to JMG's suggestion to journal, if you haven't already, might I recommend meditating on the cards/symbols of the oracle you're trying to work with? I have found that to be utterly essential to getting much of anything out of an oracle. For example, when I was first learning the Ogham, I spent 3 days meditating on each Few for 20 minutes. I'm now working my way through scrying each of the Fews - one day a week scrying, 3 days meditating on what I saw, and this has already deepened what I'm getting out of the Fews I've been able to scry so far.

Also, have you experimented at all with different spreads? When I was first learning the Runes, I tried "draw one Rune for the day" and I got pretty much nothing out of that. When I moved to a "Urd, Verdhandi, Skuld" spread (basically "past, present, future") I started having some days that worked, and lots that still left me scratching my head.

If you've already tried all this stuff, my apologies! If not, I hope it helps.

Cheers,
Jeff

comments.title: Mundane Tarot Readings
comments.content:
I did some mundane tarot readings for the U.S. Comments or interpretations are welcome from JMG and/or other forum participants/lurkers.


The first reading I asked for where the U.S. is headed over the next 6-18 months.

1. U.S. now: Death R
2. U.S. 6 months from now: 5 of Cups R
3. U.S. 18 months from now: The Hierophant
4. The Means: 5 of Pentacles
5. Silver-Lining Card: The Empress R


Note: I drew The Empress Shadow-Side for the U.S. as the silver-lining card. I called it the silver-lining because I was betting it would be a rather malefic reading and wanted to see if there was anything that could be salvaged or balanced opposite from such. For whatever karmic reasons the U.S. Silver-lining came up as Empress R.



Sri Rohit Arya says some interesting things about The Empress Shadow side (The Empress Reversed). The Empress upright is the embodiment of the Shakti energy Hinduism calls Sri. Sri is the cosmic energy of abundance. When something is at it's most abundant peak it is vibrating Sri (pronounced Shree). So already the silver-lining card is shouting the U.S citizenry experience of Abundance (Sri) is going into detriment. Maybe it's the silver-lining card because this phase is karmically necessary to deliver a nasty kick to the head that abundance unearned (which is what I consider an imperial wealth-pump to be) ends in this - The Empress R.

Sri Arya wrote of The Empress R:

Blocked and frustrated energies. Things promising abundance then fizzling out. Parturient montes, nascetur ridiculous mus - the mountains are in labor, what emerges is a ridiculous mouse. [My note: Ridiculous Mouse, thy name is The Inflation-Reduction Act].

[the french is original to Sri Arya's tarot book, I can't read or speak french]

He continues...

Creativity feels blocked or burnt out. Neglect and feeling of suffering. Excessive concerns with material comforts. Missing one's chance. Barren stage, but more in an emotional sense. Smothering love. Demanding and taxing love. Beginnings of relationship and sexual malfunction. Obsessive focus on pain of the past. Spontaneity is lost and replaced by fearful application of the rule of the book. Subconscious hostility to children, usually because having them interfered with the fulfillment of one's dreams.


In short, if I understand what he wrote. The Empress R - at least when it comes to the U.S. silver-lining - means resentments, which are already at a steady heat will likely become a rolling boil within 18 months. I keep having visions of multiple National Guard troops across many states being ordered up to put down riots by their own citizens.

***********

I then came back later and forgetting I'd already done a reading on the U.S. (oops) did a second later that day of a slightly extended timeline reading. I include it as I think the two together are saying something about the general zeitgeist of the U.S. over the next 18-36 months.

***2nd read with slightly longer time-frame*****

U.S. now: 3 of Swords
U.S. 18 months from now: 7 of Swords
U.S. 36 months from now: 5 of Swords
The Means: 2 of Cups
Silver-Lining Card: King of Wands R



I am surprised that the Means in the second reading came up as the 2 of Cups. Usually this card is one more benefically-inclined. In this reading however I think it's a direct indictment that the Inflation-Reduction Act Biden signed into law was one massive episode of collusion by both parties to loot and pillage U.S.'s taxpayers for private elite gain. I also suspect that if any other bills get signed into law it will only happen if the same kind of collusion happens again.

Sri Arya says a few extra things about 2 of Cups.

Inevitable change in strained financial circumstances.Phase of working hard without adequate rewards. Money and personal resources will be spread thin, so prioritize them. Change, especially physical relocation, which happens at the most inconvenient time. (In the long-term it plays out well, in the short-term it is very frustrating.) Trips, mostly unwelcome, for dealing with family matters. Trying too many things at once; reduce activity and focus on what's feasible. Significant inner sense of ennui or imbalance, of spiritual importance. Sense of acceptance of sense of humor as the situation plays out according to karmic plans.

I don't think his commentary overturns any of my own. Both are in play.


King of Wands Shadow side for the U.S.


Sri Rohit Arya has this to say about the characteristics of King of Wands Shadow side.


Heartbreakers, implying more than actually gets delivered. Attractiveness that slides into seductiveness, which spills over into all aspects of life. Dislikes neediness intensely and can be harsh about it. High standards are only for others. Friendships burn out quickly once their utility is over. Posturing and flamboyance to cover up inner hollowness. Attention seekers. Turn into toxic bosses hampering their employees at every turn. Spin around in dizzying creative circles but nothing gets done. Comparatively (to other cards' shadows) the card has a small Shadow side but it burns deeper!

I am quite a few years into studying ceremonial magic (a long way past any point of no return) and I've recently found out I have a personality disorder, among other things. Regardie's advice to get yourself checked out psychologically before starting magic is often repeated, but honestly, would anyone with their heart set on magic be turned aside if any issues were identified? I think it remains good advice, for what its worth, and that considering mental health and capacities before starting magic is the responsible thing to recommend; the fact remains that for many people with unusual neurology, magic is one of the only ways available to get some power over what happens to them, and their unusual talents often lead to excellence in some aspect of it or another, and perhaps some genuine success in life.

I suppose it is a case of giving fair warning and letting people wear the consequences of their own choices, once again, isn't it?

I have a question on something troubling me lately, which I'm hoping reveals a major misunderstanding somewhere, however, here it goes. On the other blog, in recent post on the Doctrine of High Magic, you wrote the following:

"Magic is the result of will and imagination acting on the astral light, the subtle energy that links all minds and things together. If you direct your will and imagination toward some goal, that action will set a current moving in the astral light; if you confirm that direction of will and imagination through actions on the material plane—yes, we can refer to these actions as “rituals”—the current becomes fixed in place, and will infallibly have an effect, either on its target or on you."

Since March of 2020 a lot of people have spent a massive amount of their time and energy imagining a catastrophic wave of death sure to arrive if people don't socially distance, mask, take experimental vaccines, etc.; many of them have spewed a great deal of vitriol at the people who refuse to play along with their fantasies, rising tolerably often to death threats; and many of the bizarre actions function as "ritual", and so this seems to fit as well.

Is it possible that the entire Covid-19 phenomena has been some kind of weird magical working?

replies.title: (no subject)
replies.content:
Yes, and in fact that's been one of my concerns all along.

My current tentative working hypothesis is that the Covid phenomenon emerged as blowback generated by the so-called Magic Resistance, the relatively large movement of people who set out to use evil magic in 2016 to reverse the results of that year's presidential election. A great many of the magical rituals worked by that movement included explicit invocations of demons. To call that a bad idea is to understate matters considerably.

The blowback took the form of a weird collective panic over an ordinary respiratory virus, Covid-19. All things considered, it was no more dangerous than an ordinary flu virus -- it has a 99.6% survival rate -- but it caused mass hysteria, primarily among the people who were most frantic about the outcome of the 2016 election. That hysteria mounted and mounted, until it convinced millions of people to rush out and get injected with experimental gene therapies, misnamed "vaccines," which had had all of eight weeks of testing and came from a family of drugs that had never been able to reach market before due to ghastly side effects.

One standard effect of magical blowback is that the people afflicted by it very often double down; they're caught up in the consciousness they themselves have generated, and proceed to run headlong toward their doom. That's what happened next. As you've pointed out, millions of people spent huge amounts of their energy in what amounts to a badly organized group ritual to conjure up mass death, and charged it with over-the-top doses of hatred and rage. That ritual hasn't harmed the unvaccinated -- we're doing just fine, thank you -- so its most likely outcome is a tidal wave of blowback toward the casters. The sole question is how soon that's going to hit. When it does, there's a real possibility that a lot of people will die in a hurry.

I hope this isn't the case. I really don't want to have to see mass death, and I know a lot of people who will die if that happens. But that's my tentative working hypothesis at this point.

Those energies in that mass magical working were being charged with the intention of mass death from Covid-19, which is exactly what will happen if a variant emerges that uses the ADE effect to its advantage.
